openingMessage: The field doesn't care how your morning started. It cares whether you're ready. Let's make sure we are.
leadershipThought: Disciplined people don't rise to the occasion — they fall back on their systems. When pressure arrives, habits take over. Build the right habits before the pressure gets here.
opsContent:
Active Jobs (2026-06-18):
• Marylou Guerra: SM Energy Schlitz A — pausing job, returning tomorrow to continue separator cleaning and NORM
• Ralph Forbes: Dropped trailer at Schlitz A
• Caleb Smith: Shull Hanks 15C #203H rescheduled — workover rig directly in work area, rerouted to Bizzell Irvin. Bizzell Irvin 15C #16 complete. MT requested follow-on reconnect at Texas Ten #3610H — waiting on forklift and permit at day's end
• Nathan Contreras: Arrived TB Ringo
• Louie Zarate: Emma Jane (poor cell service on arrival)
• Cindy Smith: Brook O
• Jacob Hernandez: Shackelford 28H complete → Shackelford 404H begun → Shackelford 405H begun
• Raymundo "Ray" Solis: Ellis complete → Robbie 17-8
• Joseph Popillion: O 32 29 #4215 — waiting to commence then job complete, headed to shop
• Jordan Lemmons: Started pumping on well
• Alex Clubb: Vessel disconnect — butterfly valve found damaged internally, vessel pulled for service, waiting on Orlando for plan
• Michael Hutt: SM Coonskin — 1500 hr pull on #152 reading 4,000 ppm H2S
• Dennis Easley: SM Energy Gipple 124H at 11,000 ppm; 154H at 3,000 ppm H2S
• Brianda Sandoval: McDaniel B Chriesman — 2 VRU units complete; moved to Brynn 1-10H Battery for 1 VRU unit, inspecting electrical
• Jimmy "Great" White: AGT Williams Nail 5H #8H with Gage

Carry-Forward:
• [ ] Butterfly valve — Bizzell area vessel: confirm resolution plan from Orlando before next visit
• [ ] SM Coonskin #152 — confirm 4,000 ppm H2S status resolved or escalated
• [ ] Gipple 124H / 154H — confirm atmospheric monitoring plan for next crew visit
• [ ] Bizzell Irvin #15 casing valve — confirm grease unit completed repair
• [ ] Texas Ten #3610H (Caleb Smith) — confirm forklift and permit secured for next day
• [ ] Marylou Guerra — SM Energy Schlitz A separator cleaning resumes tomorrow
• [ ] **Tomorrow AM: Andrews shop 5:30 AM safety meeting** — all except rig/flowback/workover (go direct to shift change). Carlsbad crew goes straight to location.
• [ ] Exxon trailer/benzene assignments confirmed for tomorrow (Ray/Anthony 607&7; Andrew/Justin F 602&2; Larry/Dermont 603&3; Ralph/Cindy 605&5; Nathan/Joseph 604&4; Caleb/Gage 606&6; Stefanie/Allen 601&1; David H/David Z 608&8; Bri trailer 272 SM Maintenance; Jordan/Baldemar contingency mid-morning)
lessonsLearned:
• Damaged butterfly valve — H2S exposure risk (Alex Clubb): Separator known for H2S — initial read 15 ppm. Crew put under supplied air for first break. Post-break re-test showed zeros on butterfly valve underbelly at AGT. Butterfly valve found damaged internally — vessel pulled for service. Waiting on Orlando for resolution plan.
• SM Coonskin #152 — 4,000 ppm H2S (Michael Hutt): 1500 hr pull reading elevated H2S. Monitor status.
• Gipple 124H / 154H — high H2S (Dennis Easley): 124H at 11,000 ppm; 154H at 3,000 ppm. Elevated atmospheric readings at both wells.
• Casing valve not holding (Caleb Smith): Bizzell Irvin #15 — casing valve issue identified, waiting on grease unit before proceeding.
• SimOps job rescheduled (Caleb Smith): Shull Hanks 15C #203H — workover rig occupying the work area, job rescheduled and crew rerouted.
exxonMoments:
Simultaneous Operations — Adjacent Crew Cross-Briefing (Operational Discipline)
When two crews share a pad, cross-brief each other's job scope and exclusion zones before anyone picks up a tool.

The disconnect got done quickly and safely before the rig got to locat (Atmospheric Monitoring)
The disconnect got done quickly and safely before the rig got to location.

Crew followed procedures verified zero energy and maintained good communica (Operational Discipline)
Crew followed procedures verified zero energy and maintained good communication

With the valve leaking we waited for a grease unit to come and fix the valv (Operational Discipline)
Did we leave the location better Yes.

Operational Discipline — 1099 Trevor Mckenzie (Operational Discipline)
Did we leave the location better Yes.

fieldNews.headline: 5 OWL reviews submitted — 2026-06-13
fieldNews.detail: Edward Carrillo, Marylou Guerra, Jacob Hernandez, Stefanie Kirschke, Raymundo Solis submitted. All checked 'left it better than we found it.'

fieldNews.headline: Edward Carrillo — Forklift operations were flawless and efficient.
Edward Carrillo — Communicate hood points more thoroughly to ensure understanding.
Marylou Guerra — Job went well, done safe.
Jacob Hernandez — Job was completed safely
Stefanie Kirschke — Everything went well during the job
Stefanie Kirschke — Just keep up the communication
Louie Zarate
fieldNews.detail: SM maintenance foreman Shane and HSE Colton visited Emma Jane and were "really happy" with vessel washouts and location cleanliness after a month on site. Colton specifically called out Louie's JSA as "very detailed" and told him to keep it up.

Butterfly valve found damaged internally — vessel pulled for service. Waiting on Orlando for resolution plan.

todaysChallenge: Before leaving a location today — ask yourself: What is one thing I can improve before I drive away?
todaysChallengeSubtext: Not because someone is watching. Because excellence is a habit. And habits become character.
